text: Following the establishment of pneumoperitoneum using an open technique, the surgeon inserts two additional 5mm laparoscopic ports under direct vision, one in the suprapubic area and the other in the epigastric region.

The surgeon employs a medial to lateral no-touch technique, initiating dissection without direct contact with the tumor, as part of the laparoscopic extended right colectomy procedure.

The surgeon retracts the ileocecal junction laterally, revealing the underlying ileocolic vessels.

The surgeon opens the mesenteric window below the ileocolic vessels, identifies the duodenum, and develops the right retrocolic space cranially and laterally along the avascular plane of Tholt.

The surgeon opens the mesenteric window immediately below the ileocolic vessels. The duodenum is identified, and the right retrocolic space is developed cranially and laterally along the avascular plane of Toldt.
The surgeon demonstrates the retrocecal appendix, visually identifying its location behind the cecum.

The surgeon approaches the ileocolic pedicle, preparing to manipulate or dissect this vascular structure within the surgical field.

The surgeon performs dissection using the Thunderbit vessel sealing device, focusing on the ileocolic vessels exposed by lateral retraction of the ileocecal junction.
The surgeon uses the Thunderbit vessel sealing device to perform dissection, continuing the medial to lateral no-touch technique on the ileocolic vessels.

The surgeon uses a combination of blunt and sharp dissection techniques while maintaining the duodenum in the operative field to prevent thermal injury during the procedure.

The surgeon delivers the resected colon specimen through a five-centimeter right-sided transverse incision, following wound protection. An unsewn end-to-side aliotransverse anastomosis is then fashioned.
